III inc superior court OF the state
of Washington. in and for the county
of Spokane.


Spokane International Railway
Company, corporation, plaintiff, VS..
James N. Allison and Allison,
his wife, defendants.


To James N. Allison and -
Allison, his wife, defendants:


You. and each of you. will please
take notice that the petitioner. the
Spokane International Railway Com
of the state of Washington. for Spo-
kane county. a petition directed to the
said court. setting forth that the Spo.-
kane International Railway Company
is corporation organized under the
laws of the state of Washington, en-
gagged in the construction and opera
ton of line of railway beginning In
the city of Spokane. thence easterly
to a point near the town of Rath-
drum. in the county of Kootenai,
state of Idaho. and thence in a
northeasterly direction up the valley
of the Mooyie river, to the interna-
tional boundary line between the
United States and Canada: that it Is
essential for the operation Of its lines
of railway. and the accommodation
of the public using the same. that II
acquire those lands situate in Spo-
kane county. Washington. and de-
scribed as lot fourteen (tD in block
five 60 of River Front Addition to
Spokane Falls (now Spokane), state
of Washington. for use for it's right
of way for tho line of it's said rail-
way, and for necessary sidetracks.
depot grounds and terminal yards
that the defendant, James n. Allison.
appears to be the sole owner in fee
of said realty. ana tho defendant


- Allison. his wife, has or claims
to have some interest in or lien upon
said premises: and that said petition
prays that the court empanel a jury
to ascertain and determine the COM
pensation to be made in money to
the owner of said realty by petitioner
for the purposes aforesaid:





hereby notified that on the 18th day
of July, 1905, petitioner will apply
to the superior court of the state OF
Washington. for Spokane county. and
to the Honorable William A. Huneke
Judge thereof. at his courtroom In
the city OF Spokane. at the hour OF
$50 a. M, or as soon thereafter as
counsel for petitioner can be heard,
for hearing upon said petition, and
an adjudication by the court that the
taking of said realty is for public use.
and is necessary to the business of
petitioner, and for tho liking of a
be called to determine the compensa-
ton TO be made therefor.
